jd.title: Principal Systems Engineer — Airborne Platforms
jd.text:
Principal Systems Engineer — Airborne Platforms
BAE Systems — Air Sector
Warton, Lancashire, UK (hybrid: 3 days on-site)

About BAE Systems Air:
BAE Systems Air sector delivers some of the world's most advanced defense air platforms, including Typhoon, Hawk, and the Future Combat Air System (FCAS/Tempest). We are at the forefront of next-generation combat aircraft development.

Role Summary:
We are looking for a Principal Systems Engineer to lead systems integration activities on our airborne platform programs. You will work across the full systems engineering lifecycle — from requirements capture through to integration, test, and certification — ensuring architectural coherence across complex avionics and mission systems.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead systems architecture definition and integration for avionics and mission systems
- Conduct requirements engineering using DOORS or equivalent (JAMA, Polarion)
- Apply MBSE techniques (SysML, Cameo/MagicDraw) for system modeling and analysis
- Plan and execute V&V activities including system-level ground testing and support to flight test
- Drive interface management and configuration control across subsystem boundaries
- Conduct trade studies, system performance analysis, and technical risk assessment
- Support airworthiness and certification activities (DEFSTAN 00-970, Mil-Std-882)
- Work with integrated project teams across multiple sites and international partners
- Contribute to technology maturation for next-gen combat air concepts
- Mentor systems engineering practitioners and contribute to SE capability development

Required Qualifications:
- Degree in Systems Engineering, Aerospace, Electrical, or related engineering discipline
- 10+ years of systems engineering experience in aerospace/defense
- Demonstrated experience with MBSE tools and techniques
- Strong requirements engineering background (DOORS or equivalent)
- Experience with V&V planning and execution on complex platforms
- Understanding of avionics architectures (sensors, EW, mission computing, datalinks)
- INCOSE CSEP or working towards it
- UK SC clearance or ability to obtain

Desirable:
- Experience with fighter aircraft programs
- Knowledge of open architecture standards (MOSA, FACE, SOSA)
- Flight test support experience
- Experience with DO-178C/DO-254 certification
- International collaboration experience
- Teaching or mentoring experience in systems engineering

We offer:
- Competitive salary + pension (up to 14% employer contribution)
- Flexible working, compressed hours available
- Share incentive plan
- Annual bonus scheme

key_matches: MBSE: Cameo Systems Modeler (directly listed in CV)
IBM Rational DOORS for requirements engineering (directly listed)
V&V planning and execution (core competency listed)
Avionics architecture knowledge: mission computing, EW, sensors (deep JF-17 experience)
Fighter aircraft programs: JF-17 Thunder (directly relevant to Typhoon/FCAS context)
Flight test support experience (participated in campaigns)
Teaching SE at NUTECH and College of Aero Eng (mentoring requirement met)
gaps_to_bridge: UK SC clearance needed — bridge: 'held equivalent military clearance, SC obtainable for sponsored candidates'
No DEFSTAN 00-970 experience — bridge: 'familiar with MIL-STD equivalent, DEFSTAN adaptation straightforward'
No open architecture standards (MOSA, FACE, SOSA) — bridge: 'familiar with modular architecture principles from indigenous mission computer development'
No SysML mentioned explicitly (has Cameo which implies SysML) — bridge: clarify Cameo = SysML tool
No trade study experience listed explicitly — bridge: 'conducted system-level analysis during design rig integration'
expected_output_notes.position_assessment: Strong position — near-complete match, SE is core career, fighter aircraft experience directly relevant
cv_sections_to_emphasize: Lead Systems Integration Expert at CADI (Role #4) — design rig, V&V, requirements
Integrated Systems & Design Specialist at PAC Kamra (Role #1) — mission computer, subsystem development
Deputy Director PMO (Role #7) — FMECA/LORA/RCM, configuration management
Current teaching role — SE instruction maps to mentoring requirement
